reason: Answer B captures all key points: Goodhart's law dynamic, the math showing activated users are roughly flat despite 2x signups, the pay-on-activation fix, segmentation (referred vs organic, and referrer quality/fraud), and reframes success metric as cost-per-activated-user. Answer A is correct but thin — it only hits two of the four key points (reward-hunters, pay-on-activation) and omits the crucial "measure net activated users" and "segment referrers" insights, which are central to giving decision-relevant guidance. B's length is functional, not padding — each addition is a distinct actionable point.
